behaviors.description: A cell containing `=SUM(A1:A10)` evaluates to the correct sum of the values in cells A1 through A10.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is function semantics for SUM over a range, reproducible inside evaluator/function code with fixed cell inputs. No required module-boundary handoff. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no OS/network/subprocess dependence. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text only asserts arithmetic correctness of one formula (`=SUM(A1:A10)`), not cross-module orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Call formula evaluator/function implementation with a prepared range A1..A10 containing representative values (ints, floats, negatives) and assert exact numeric result for `SUM` range evaluation.

behaviors.description: A cell referencing another cell that contains a formula (e.g., `=B1+1` where B1 is `=A1*2`) produces the correct cascaded result.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears at module handoff between reference resolution, dependency evaluation, and formula execution across multiple cells. Unit on one function risks missing stale/intermediate value propagation. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required, (b) no AI output, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM orchestration. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text is purely workbook-internal cascade (`B1 is =A1*2`, `=B1+1`) with no external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Create a workbook with A1 value, B1 formula `=A1*2`, C1 formula `=B1+1`; run full in-process recalculation path and assert C1 equals cascaded expected value and updates correctly when A1 changes.

behaviors.description: The engine detects a circular dependency (A1 references B1, B1 references A1) and returns a circular reference error instead of looping.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure boundary is interaction of dependency graph construction and evaluator execution/termination behavior; single-function tests can miss runtime loop-prevention integration. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no OS/network/subprocess, no AI dependency, no multi-agentic chain. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text only concerns internal cycle detection and error return (`A1 references B1, B1 references A1`).
behaviors.test_description: Build two-cell cyclic formulas in a workbook, invoke normal evaluation entrypoint, assert a circular-reference error object/code is returned within bounded time and no infinite loop occurs.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP with an exact match returns the correct value from the lookup table, and returns an error when the key is not found.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is function-level lookup semantics (match/not-found branches) and can be reproduced with deterministic local table inputs. No required cross-module state handoff. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real infra needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text targets built-in function correctness for one formula family (VLOOKUP exact match).
behaviors.test_description: Invoke VLOOKUP evaluator with an in-memory table/range fixture, assert returned value for existing key and correct error token/code for missing key.

behaviors.description: The IF function correctly handles nested conditions (e.g., `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")`).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is expression/function logic for nested branching, reproducible in evaluator with fixed inputs. No mandatory module interaction beyond local expression evaluation. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: none. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text specifies nested IF semantics, not dependency-graph orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate nested IF formula against boundary inputs for A1 (e.g., 10, 11, 20, 21) and assert outputs `low`/`medium`/`high` exactly match branch rules.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins cell values with the correct separator and handles empty cells by skipping them rather than inserting blank strings.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is built-in function string-assembly behavior with deterministic inputs, testable at single evaluator/function boundary. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no external infra. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on CONCATENATE output formatting and empty-cell handling only.
behaviors.test_description: Run CONCATENATE evaluation with fixture operands including non-empty and empty cells plus separator, assert output excludes empty-cell contributions and keeps separator placement as specified.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing a cell in a different sheet (e.g., `=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves correctly when the referenced sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure occurs at cross-module boundary between workbook/sheet namespace resolution and evaluator; single-function test may not catch sheet lookup wiring errors. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no OS/network/subprocess requirement, no AI output dependence, no multi-agentic sequence. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text requires only in-process sheet existence and reference resolution (`Sheet2!A1`).
behaviors.test_description: Create multi-sheet workbook with value on Sheet2!A1 and formula on Sheet1 referencing it; execute normal recalc path and assert resolved numeric result.

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell's value changes, all dependent formulas are recalculated in the correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is specifically about interaction of dependency tracking, invalidation, and scheduler/evaluator ordering; a unit test of one function cannot expose wrong cross-module propagation order. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; no AI dependency; no agentic chain. Rejection gate: rejected System because ordering failure is internal graph recomputation, not external infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Set up dependency graph (fan-out and multi-level chains), mutate a source cell, run recalc, and assert updated values match expected results that require correct topological evaluation order.

behaviors.description: The undo operation after editing a formula restores both the formula text and all previously computed dependent values.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure boundary is state handoff among edit buffer/model, history stack, and recalculation engine; unit tests on history object alone can miss dependent-value restoration regressions. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no OS/network/subprocess required; no AI model output; no multi-agentic sequence. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text is internal undo/recalc state restoration, not OS-level behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Edit a formula that changes downstream cells, verify changed state, perform undo through public engine API, then assert formula text and all affected dependent cell values are restored to pre-edit snapshot.

behaviors.description: Saving a workbook to disk and loading it back produces identical cell values and formulas — no data loss or precision change in floating-point results.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is serializer/deserializer plus workbook model interaction (round-trip fidelity), which requires multi-module wiring. REAL-INFRA: local filesystem use is sufficient and fits Integration here; behavior does not require OS-specific file-event semantics/subprocess/network. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real network/subprocess/OS edge beyond local file I/O, (b) no AI dependency, (c) no agentic chain. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text is about binary-format round-trip correctness and precision, not OS-specific failure modes.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with formulas and floating-point edge values, save via production binary writer to a temp local file, reload via production reader, and assert exact formula text equality plus value equality within strict precision policy.
